sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: ὦ μέλεος ἥβης σῆς , Ὀρέστα , καὶ πότμου
sections.translation: Alas for your unhappy youth , Orestes , and for your fated

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: θανάτου τʼ ἀώρου . ζῆν ἐχρῆν σʼ , ὅτʼ οὐκέτʼ εἶ .
sections.translation: untimely death ! When you should have lived , you are going to die .

sections.label: Section 1031
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν μοι περιβάλῃς ἀνανδρίαν ,
sections.translation: By the gods , do not unman me , bringing me to tears by the recollection of my sorrows .

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: θανούμεθʼ · οὐχ οἷόν τε μὴ στένειν κακά .
sections.translation: We are about to die ; it is not possible for me not to grieve over our troubles ; it is a piteous thing for all men to lose life , that is so sweet .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: τόδʼ ἦμαρ ἡμῖν κύριον · δεῖ δʼ ἢ βρόχους
sections.translation: This is the day appointed for us ; we must fit the dangling noose about our necks or whet the sword for use .

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: σύ νύν μʼ , ἀδελφέ , μή τις Ἀργείων κτάνῃ
sections.translation: You be the one to kill me , brother , so that no Argive may insult Agamemnon ’ s son by my death .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: ἅλις τὸ μητρὸς αἷμʼ ἔχω · σὲ δʼ οὐ κτενῶ ,
sections.translation: Enough that I have a mother ’ s blood upon me ; I will not kill you ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτόχειρι θνῇσχʼ ὅτῳ βούλῃ τρόπῳ .
sections.translation: but die by your own hand , however you wish .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · οὐδὲν σοῦ ξίφους λελείψομαι .
sections.translation: Agreed ; I will not be behind you in using the sword ; only I long to throw my arms about your neck .

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: τέρπου κενὴν ὄνησιν , εἰ τερπνὸν τόδε
sections.translation: Enjoy that empty satisfaction , if embraces have any joy for those who have come so near to death .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ὦ ποθεινὸν ἥδιστόν τʼ ἔχων
sections.translation: My dearest , you who have a name that sounds most loved and sweet to your sister , partner in one soul with her !

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ἔκ τοί με τήξεις · καί σʼ ἀμείψασθαι θέλω
sections.translation: Oh , you will melt my heart ! I want to give you back a fond embrace . And why should such a wretch as I still feel any shame ? Embracing Electra Heart to heart , my sister ! how sweet to me this close embrace !

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: τάδʼ ἀντὶ παίδων καὶ γαμηλίου λέχους —
sections.translation: In place of children and the marriage bed this greeting is all that is possible to us poor sufferers .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! If only the same sword , if it is right , could kill us both , and one coffin of cedar-wood receive us !

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.original: ἥδιστʼ ἂν εἴη ταῦθʼ · ὁρᾷς δὲ δὴ φίλων
sections.translation: That would be very sweet ; but surely you see

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ὡς ἐσπανίσμεθʼ , ὥστε κοινωνεῖν τάφου .
sections.translation: we are too destitute of friends to be allowed one tomb between us .

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.original: οὐδʼ εἶφʼ ὑπὲρ σοῦ , μὴ θανεῖν σπουδὴν ἔχων ,
sections.translation: Did that coward Menelaus , that traitor to my father , not even speak for you , or make an effort to save your life ?

sections.label: Section 1058
sections.original: οὐδʼ ὄμμʼ ἔδειξεν , ἀλλʼ ἐπὶ σκήπτροις ἔχων
sections.translation: He did not even show himself , but , with his hopes centered on the throne , he was careful not to attempt the rescue of his friends .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶʼ ὅπως γενναῖα καὶ Ἀγαμέμνονος
sections.translation: But let us see how we may die a noble death , one most worthy of Agamemnon . I , for my part , will let the city see my noble spirit when I plunge the sword to my heart , and you in turn must imitate my daring .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: Πυλάδη , σὺ δʼ ἡμῖν τοῦ φόνου γενοῦ βραβεύς ,
sections.translation: Pylades , be the arbitrator of our slaughter and , when we both are dead , lay out our bodies decently ; carry them to our father ’ s grave and bury us there with him . Farewell , now ; I am leaving for the deed , as you see .

sections.label: Section 1069
sections.original: ἐπίσχες . ἓν μὲν πρῶτά σοι μομφὴν ἔχω ,
sections.translation: Stop ! there is first one point I have to blame you for ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: εἰ ζῆν με χρῄζειν σοῦ θανόντος ἤλπισας .
sections.translation: if you thought I would care to live when you are dead .

sections.label: Section 1071
sections.original: τί γὰρ προσήκει κατθανεῖν σʼ ἐμοῦ μέτα ;
sections.translation: But why are you called on to die with me ?

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: ἤρου ; τί δὲ ζῆν σῆς ἑταιρίας ἄτερ ;
sections.translation: Do you ask ? What is life to me without your companionship ?

sections.label: Section 1073
sections.original: οὐκ ἔκτανες σὴν μητέρʼ , ὡς ἐγὼ τάλας .
sections.translation: You did not kill your mother , as I did to my sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1074
sections.original: σὺν σοί γε κοινῇ · ταὐτὰ καὶ πάσχειν με δεῖ .
sections.translation: At least I helped you ; and so I ought to suffer the same penalty .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: ἀπόδος τὸ σῶμα πατρί , μὴ σύνθνῃσκέ μοι .
sections.translation: Surrender to your father , do not die with me . You still have a city , while I no longer have , and your father ’ s home , and a great refuge of wealth . You have failed to marry my poor sister , whom I betrothed to you from a deep regard for your companionship ;

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἄλλο λέκτρον παιδοποίησαι λαβών ,
sections.translation: but find another bride and rear a family ; for the marriage-tie which bound us is no more . Farewell , be happy , my beloved friend ; we cannot , but you may ; for we , the dead , are robbed of happiness .

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ἦ πολὺ λέλειψαι τῶν ἐμῶν βουλευμάτων .
sections.translation: How far you are from grasping what I mean ! May the fruitful earth , the radiant sky refuse to hold my blood , if ever I turn traitor and desert you when I have freed myself . For I shared in the murder , which I will not deny ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: καὶ πάντʼ ἐβούλευσʼ ὧν σὺ νῦν τίνεις δίκας ·
sections.translation: and also schemed the whole plot , for which you are now paying the penalty ; and so I ought to die together with you and her . For I consider her , whom you betrothed to me , as my wife . Whatever shall I say , when I reach Delphi , the citadel of Phocis ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: ὃς πρὶν μὲν ὑμᾶς δυστυχεῖν φίλος παρῆ ,
sections.translation: if I was your friend before your misfortunes , but ceased to be your friend , when you were unfortunate ? That must not be ; no , this concerns me , too . But since we are to die , let us take counsel together that Menelaus may share our misfortune .

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , εἰ γὰρ τοῦτο κατθάνοιμʼ ἰδών .
sections.translation: Best of friends ! if only I could see this before I die .

sections.label: Section 1101
sections.original: πιθοῦ νυν , ἄμμεινόν τε φασγάνου τομάς .
sections.translation: Listen to me , and delay the stroke of the sword .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: μενῶ , τὸν ἐχθρὸν εἴ τι τιμωρήσομαι .
sections.translation: I will , if I may take vengeance on my enemy .

sections.label: Section 1103
sections.original: σίγα νυν · ὡς γυναιξὶ πιστεύω βραχύ .
sections.translation: Hush now ! I have small confidence in women .

sections.label: Section 1104
sections.original: μηδὲν τρέσῃς τάσδʼ · ὡς πάρεισʼ ἡμῖν φίλαι .
sections.translation: Have no fear of these ; for they are our friends who are here .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: Ἑλένην κτάνωμεν , Μενέλεῳ λύπην πικράν .
sections.translation: Let us kill Helen , a bitter grief to Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.original: πῶς ; τὸ γὰρ ἕτοιμον ἔστιν , εἴ γʼ ἔσται καλῶς .
sections.translation: How ? I am ready , if there is any chance of success .

sections.label: Section 1107
sections.original: σφάξαντες . ἐν δόμοις δὲ κρύπτεται σέθεν .
sections.translation: With our swords ; she is hiding in your house .

sections.label: Section 1108
sections.original: μάλιστα · καὶ δὴ πάντʼ ἀποσφραγίζεται .
sections.translation: Indeed she is ; and already she is putting her seal on everything .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκέθʼ , Ἅιδην νυμφίον κεκτημένη .
sections.translation: No longer , after she is married to Hades .

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ; ἔχει γὰρ βαρβάρους ὀπάονας .
sections.translation: But how ? She has her barbarian attendants .

sections.label: Section 1111
sections.original: τίνας ; Φρυγῶν γὰρ οὐδένʼ ἂν τρέσαιμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Barbarians indeed ! I am not the man to fear any Phrygian .

sections.label: Section 1112
sections.original: οἵους ἐνόπτρων καὶ μύρων ἐπιστάτας .
sections.translation: They are only fit to look after mirrors and perfumes !

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: τρυφὰς γὰρ ἥκει δεῦρʼ ἔχουσα Τρωικάς ;
sections.translation: Has she brought Trojan luxury with her here ?

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: ὥσθʼ Ἑλλὰς αὐτῇ σμικρὸν οἰκητήριον .
sections.translation: So much so , that Hellas is too small for her to live in .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: οὐδὲν τὸ δοῦλον πρὸς τὸ μὴ δοῦλον γένος .
sections.translation: The race of slaves is nothing to those who are free .

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τόδʼ ἔρξας δὶς θανεῖν οὐχ ἅζομαι .
sections.translation: Well , if I can do this deed , I do not shrink from dying twice over .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδʼ ἐγὼ μήν , σοί γε τιμωρούμενος .
sections.translation: No , nor I either , if it is you I am avenging .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: τὸ πρᾶγμα δήλου καὶ πέραινʼ , ὅπως λέγεις .
sections.translation: Explain the matter , and continue describing your plan .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: ἔσιμεν ἐς οἴκους δῆθεν ὡς θανούμενοι .
sections.translation: We will enter the house on the pretence of going to our death .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: ἔχω τοσοῦτον , τἀπίλοιπα δʼ οὐκ ἔχω .
sections.translation: So far I follow you , but not beyond .

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original: γόους πρὸς αὐτὴν θησόμεσθʼ ἃ πάσχομεν .
sections.translation: We will lament our sufferings to her .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐκδακρῦσαί γʼ ἔνδοθεν κεχαρμένην .
sections.translation: So that she will shed tears , although her heart is glad .

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: καὶ νῷν παρέσται ταῦθʼ ἅπερ κείνῃ τότε .
sections.translation: And our condition will be like hers .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ἔπειτʼ ἀγῶνα πῶς ἀγωνιούμεθα ;
sections.translation: How shall we proceed next in our contest ?

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: κρύπτʼ ἐν πέπλοισι τοισίδʼ ἕξομεν ξίφη .
sections.translation: We shall have swords concealed in our cloaks .

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: πρόσθεν δʼ ὀπαδῶν τίς ὄλεθρος γενήσεται ;
sections.translation: Will we dispose of her attendants first ?

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: ἐκκλῄσομεν σφᾶς ἄλλον ἄλλοσε στέγης .
sections.translation: We will shut them up in different parts of the house .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: καὶ τόν γε μὴ σιγῶντʼ ἀποκτείνειν χρεών .
sections.translation: And whoever refuses to be quiet , we must kill .
